title: Audrey Bergner and Aurelia Jeffery Exploring the Dense Andean Forests Near Bariloche Rio Negro
description: Bariloche, Río Negro: Audrey Bergner and Aurelia Jeffery pause during a transit through the lush Andean temperate rainforest during their Cruce Andino journey. The ambient, overcast lighting filters through the dense canopy of native Nothofagus trees, softening the textures of the plush, fleece layers worn to combat the crisp mountain air. Audrey offers a joyful expression while holding Aurelia, whose curious and contemplative gaze captures the wonder of exploring Patagonia’s rugged, remote wilderness. A wide-angle perspective emphasizes the intimacy of their bond against the backdrop of an untamed landscape that defines the border region between Argentina and Chile. The shallow depth of field isolates the subjects from the textured trunks and verdant foliage, drawing focus to their expressions amidst the vast wilderness. This candid moment serves as a testament to the accessibility of adventure travel even in the most remote corners of the Southern Hemisphere. As the wind rustles the leaves, the pair remains bundled in high-quality outdoor gear, perfectly suited for the unpredictable climate of the Andes. Their presence highlights the importance of fostering a deep connection to nature through immersive, cross-border expeditions that link communities across the mountain range. This scene illustrates the transformative power of family travel, where shared discovery shapes the identity of the next generation of explorers. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
